_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Hiatus hernia, including Type 1 (sliding), Type 2 (paraesophageal), Type 3 (mixed), or Type 4 (giant).
Symptoms of reflux (regurgitation, heartburn) refractory to conservative and medical treatment.

Complications of sliding hernia or gastroesophageal reflux (e.g., stricture, severe ulceration, fibrosis, esophagitis, chronic occult GI blood loss with anemia, lower esophageal stricture, Barrett's esophagus, adenocarcinoma, aspiration pneumonia).
Complete mechanical failure of the lower esophageal sphincter (LES).
Paraesophageal hiatus hernia to prevent severe complications (e.g., hemorrhage, incarceration, obstruction, strangulation).
Symptoms due to complications such as dysphagia, odynophagia, haematemesis, melaena, asthma, chest pain, fullness after eating, upper abdominal pain, pressure sensation in lower chest.

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes - detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, indication for procedure?
Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was the imaging indicative of surgery?

common_queries: Clinical notes incomplete (missing detailed history, signs & symptoms, planned line of treatment, or indication for procedure).
Imaging (e.g., Upper GI series/barium swallow, Upper endoscopy, chest x-ray) not confirming diagnosis or not indicative of surgery.
Clinical presentation not indicative of surgery.
Mandatory investigation reports (24-hr pH-monitoring, Oesophageal manometry) missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or incomplete, or daily vitals/line of treatment not documented at claim stage.
Detailed Procedure / Operative Notes missing or incomplete at claim stage.
Discharge summary missing or lacking follow-up advice.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Care at Tertiary Hospital; laparoscopic facility for laparoscopic procedures
clinical_key_pointers: Type 1 - sliding or oroesophagogastric hernia, in which the gastroesophageal junction slides into the thorax
Type 2 - Paraesophageal or Rolling, in which a portion of the stomach (usually the fundus) is insinuated next to the esophagus inside the gastroesophageal junction in the hiatus

Type 3 - Mixed hernia, combination of sliding and paraesophageal types. The fundus or body of the stomach or both have herniated into the chest; the gastro-oesophageal junction is also herniated into the chest, but rests below the herniated stomach
Type 4 - Giant hiatus hernia (massive herniation), Occurrence of any type of hiatus hernia along with herniation of one or more other organs, such as colon, small bowel, omentum, and spleen.
surgical_options: Rosetti-Nissen fundoplication (360-degree fundic wrap without division of the short gastric vessels)
Partial fundoplication (Toupet - 270-degree posterior wrap for patients with severe associated motor abnormalities)
